operators.name: Wave CHOP
operators.summary: The Wave CHOP makes repeating waves with a variety of shapes. It is by default 10-seconds of 1-second sine waves, a total of 600 frames. You can adjust period (frequency), phase, shape, amplitude and offset. The Wave CHOP gives a set of waves in channels of a specifiable time-range. It is superceded by the LFO CHOP which gives an endless stream of waves that is Time Sliced , and the Pattern CHOP that has more control over shaping waveform samples (and is time-independent). The Audio Oscillator CHOP also creates waveforms that are continually repeated, but its defaults are for higher-frequency waves at higher (audio) sample rates. Because the Extend Conditions of the Wave CHOP are set to Repeat, the wave will repeat outside the 10-second range. Applied to the actual waveform can be an offset, decay and ramp. Multiple channels can be generated in the Channel Name parameter using Pattern Expansion . A few examples of name pattern name expansion: See also: LFO CHOP , Pattern CHOP , Audio Oscillator CHOP . waveCHOP_Class

parameters.description: ⊞ - There is a choice of waveforms shapes:
The period is the number of seconds, frames or samples that the waveform repeats in. It is expressed in the chop's Units (default is Seconds), found on the Common page.
Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.
The phase shifts the waveform in time, and is expressed as a fraction of a period, usually between 0 and 1.
You can vary the shape of some of the waveform types by changing the bias within the range -1 to +1.
The waveform's value can be offset. A sine wave can remain always positive by setting Offset to 1.
The wave's value can be scaled.

The wave's amplitude can be reduced over time with an "exponential decay". For example, if the Decay is 0.2 and the Units are seconds, then the amplitude will decay to 0.8 after 1 second, and 0.8 of 0.8 (or 0.64) after 2 seconds.
Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

Then a ramp is added to the result with a slope of Ramp. The channel increases by the Ramp Slope value every Unit of time. For example, if Ramp is 1.2, the channel increases by 1.2 every second, in addition to the shape of the wave.
Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

If the waveform type is Expression , the Expression parameter is used to input a math expression. Some local variables are available: $I (Index), $L (the loop variable over the period 0 to 1), $C (the cycle variable, the integer number of cycles the waveform has passed at the current index).

You can creates many channels with simple patterns like " chan[1-20] ", which generates 20 channels from chan1 to chan20. See the section, Common CHOP Parameters for a description of this and all Options. See Scope and Channel Name Matching Options.
Start of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).
Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.
End of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).
Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.
The sample rate of the channels, in samples per second.
⊞ - The left extend conditions (before/after range).
⊞ - The right extend conditions (before/after range).
The value used for the Default Value extend condition.
Turning this on forces the channels to be " Time Sliced ". A Time Slice is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.
To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a Scope string on the Common page.

⊞ - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

operators.name: wrnchAI CHOP
operators.summary: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro . Note: Wrnch has be acquired by another company and it is no longer possible to obtain a license to use it anymore. This CHOP has been removed in 2022.20000+ builds. Using the wrnchAI engine to track human motion in any video stream, this CHOP will output channels for Body, Face and Hand positions. Multiple people can be tracked at the same time with low-latency, and the system compatible with all cameras and video feeds. TouchDesigner used the wrnchAI Edge SDK and all computation is done locally in realtime utilizing Nvidia GPUs and the NVIDIA CUDA® Deep Neural Network library ( cuDNN ). This can offer skeletal tracking of multiple people at 60fps or higher (90-120fps etc). Once you have a wrnchAI license (purchased or trial), you will have access to your wrnchAI licenses page and the wrnchAI Edge Releases page. The wrnchAI CHOP will take a few moments to initialize and then will be ready for tracking. Initializing is only required when a new Model Folder is introduced, it will not need to re-initialize each time the project is started. wrnchaiCHOP_Class

parameters.description: Add the Edge License Key from your wrnchAI account here. Requires a separate license from wrnchAI .
Specify the path the the folder that contains the trained models. See Quickstart Guide above for details.
⊞ - A menu of available GPU (s) to run wrnchAI on. Selecting 'Default' uses the same GPU TouchDesigner is currently running on.
Specify the TOP to process for tracking.
Enable the channels (tx, ty, tz) for body tracking points in 3D space.
Enable the channels (u, v) for body tracking points in 2D.
Enable the IK channels (tx, ty, tz, rx, ry, rz and associated roll channels) for body tracking points in 3D space.
Enable the position and bounds channels (u, v, width, height) for faces.
Enable the position channels (u, v) for all face tracking points.
Enable the position and bounds channels (u, v, width, height) for hands.
Enable the position channels (u, v) for all hand tracking points.

Sets the maximum number of people that can be tracked. For each person/player a new set of channels is created prefixed p1, p2, p3, ... etc. NOTE : The parameter slider goes to 10 but higher numbers of players can be tracked by setting a higher Max Player value.
Adjusts the values of u and v channels to take the TOP 's aspect ratio into account. When using non-square input TOP , turn this on to line up u and v position with the image.
Turning this on forces the channels to be " Time Sliced ". A Time Slice is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.
To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a Scope string on the Common page.

operators.name: ZED CHOP
operators.summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system. The ZED CHOP reads positional and plane tracking from the ZED camera. It can additionally use a UV to find plane in the room, and return information about that plane such as it's size/orientation either relative to the camera, or the room itself. Additionally, the ZED CHOP can do skeleton tracking by enabling the 'Body Tracking' menu. NOTE: This CHOP works with the Stereolabs ZED hardware. For more information and to know what ZED SDK to install refer to the ZED article. See also ZED TOP and ZED SOP . zedCHOP_Class

parameters.description: When 'On' data is captured from the ZED camera.
The name of the primary ZED TOP that is configuring the camera. The primary TOP controls which camera the CHOP receives data from.
Enables transformation, which returns the position and rotation of the camera.
Zeros out the current camera position, effectively makes the current position the new origin.
Enables plane at point, which returns the position of the plane corresponding the UV coordinate.

Returns the camera position relative to the initial position of the camera. A tx , ty , tz triplet of channels will be output for the position of the camera in meters. An rx , ry , rz triplet will be output for the orientation.
Instantly returns the camera position relative to the initial position of the camera.
⊞ - Controls if the panel coordinates will be given relative to the camera or the world.
Sets the U coordinate of the point in the image to extract plane position.
Sets the V coordinate of the point in the image to extract plane position.
Returns tx , ty , and tz position channels of the center of the plane.
Returns rx , ry , and rz rotation channels for the plane.
Returns nx , ny , and nz normal channels of the plane.
Returns size of the bounding rectangle of the plane.

⊞ - Body tracking allows for tracking human skeletons that are found in the video frame. Each mode uses an AI model that will need to be trained one for your GPU . This will take several minutes, but only needs to be done once per model per machine, as long as the GPU isn't changed. For models where the 'Joint Mode' parameter is disabled, the outputs will be 'Absolute'.
The maximum number of bodies that will be tracked.
Enable to output 3D positions. Rotations will be included if the Joint Mode is set to 'Relative'.
⊞ - Controls if the joint values are given in absolute in scene or relative to each other.
2D normalized UV coordinates for each joint, giving their position within the image.

Corrects the UV coordinate using the aspect ratio. For a 2x1 aspect ratio, a normalized coordinate of (0.25, 0.25) will cover a quarter of the width and an eighth of the height, not a quarter of the height. This mode is useful when performing orthographic rendering or using the Fraction Aspect unit within TOPs.
Enable bounding boxes. Head bounding box is only enabled when Body 3D is enabled.
Turning this on forces the channels to be " Time Sliced ". A Time Slice is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.
